subject: Computer Networking
title: HTTP — The Web's Protocol
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on HTTP — The Web's Protocol.

hook.text: When you click a link, your browser sends an HTTP request. The server sends back an HTTP response. That's the web.

buildup.text: HTTP methods define actions: GET fetches data, POST submits data, PUT updates, DELETE removes.

discovery.text: Status codes tell the outcome: 200 OK, 301 Moved, 404 Not Found, 500 Server Error.

twist.text: HTTP is stateless. The server forgets you after each request. Cookies are the workaround for sessions.

climax.text: Headers carry metadata: content type, caching rules, auth tokens. They're invisible but control everything.

punchline.text: HTTP is request and response. The simplest protocol that runs the web.

quiz.question: Why is HTTP called stateless?
options: The server does not remember previous requests
It cannot transfer files larger than 1 MB
It only works over wireless connections
